data.title: As a team using DSO with Jira, locally-created relates_to links appear as Relates links in Jira
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add LINK event processing to bridge-outbound.py so that local relates_to relationships are synced to Jira as "Relates" links via name-based link type lookup.
**Why**: Currently, locally-created relates_to relationships are invisible in Jira. This bridges that gap so cross-epic coordination links are visible to all team members regardless of which tool they use.
**Scope**:
- IN: LINK event handler in process_outbound, name-based link type resolution (GET /issueLinkType to find "Relates" by name with cached ID), pre-create dedup check (query existing Jira links before creating), graceful degradation on Jira rejection (bridge_alert without blocking), 404-on-source-issue treated as "already gone"
- OUT: UNLINK event handling, round-trip verification, AcliClient method additions (handled by this story as implementation detail but specified at the behavior level)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a LINK event with relation "relates_to" in the git diff stream causes bridge-outbound.py to create a corresponding "Relates" link in Jira between the two mapped issues
  <- Satisfies: "bridge-outbound.py processes LINK events: maps local relates_to to Jira Relates link type via name-based lookup"
- When this story is complete, the Jira "Relates" link type ID is resolved by name (not hardcoded) and cached for the duration of a sync run
  <- Satisfies: "name-based lookup (GET /issueLinkType to find Relates by name, cache resolved ID; never hardcode link type ID)"
- When this story is complete, duplicate LINK events for the same source-target-relates_to triple do not create duplicate Jira links
  <- Satisfies: "LINK event sync is idempotent: duplicate events for the same (source, target, relates_to) triple do not create duplicate Jira links"
- When this story is complete, if Jira rejects the link creation, a bridge_alert is written and the sync run continues without blocking
  <- Satisfies: "Jira rejection handled gracefully: writes bridge_alert and persist_relationship_rejection, does not block local link creation or other sync operations"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Name-based link type lookup may fail if "Relates" type is not found -- log enumerated types for admin configuration and write bridge_alert
- [Reliability] Pre-create dedup requires querying existing Jira links -- AcliClient needs get_issue_links and get_issue_link_types methods
- [Testing] All Jira API interactions must be mock-compatible
